architecture_summary: Shared Siamese encoder with explicit change fusion by concatenating before, after, and absolute-difference features at the bottleneck only
result_summary: Slight new overall leader; rerun confirmed the bottleneck concat+absdiff recipe is both strong and reproducible
comparison_note: Edges out the previous best by a small margin and reinforces this family as the safest top-performing choice

architecture_summary: Shared Siamese encoder with explicit change fusion by concatenating before, after, and absolute-difference features at the bottleneck only
result_summary: Second rerun nearly matched the leader and further confirmed that the best bottleneck recipe is highly reproducible
comparison_note: Three independent runs now cluster at the top, so future gains likely need training or data improvements rather than architectural changes

architecture_summary: Shared Siamese encoder with explicit change fusion by concatenating before, after, and absolute-difference features at the bottleneck only
result_summary: Original breakout run for the leading architecture; still one of the three strongest results overall
comparison_note: Early stopping worked well here and this run established the architecture that subsequent reruns confirmed

architecture_summary: Post-disaster image only with a single encoder and no explicit before/after fusion; joint segmentation plus tile-level classification head
result_summary: Near-tied to the leaders; rerun plus early stopping turned the simple after-only model into a real contender
comparison_note: Nearly matches the best Siamese run with a simpler setup, but the weaker rerun B suggests this recipe is less consistent

architecture_summary: Post-disaster image only with a single encoder and no explicit before/after fusion; joint segmentation plus tile-level classification head
result_summary: Highest classification F1 among the major contenders and still a strong simple-model baseline
comparison_note: Shows the post-disaster image alone is surprisingly competitive, though reruns suggest it is less stable than the best Siamese bottleneck model

architecture_summary: Shared Siamese encoder with simple before/after concatenation only at the bottleneck
result_summary: Most stable of the top pre-early-stopping models and still one of the strongest overall
comparison_note: Very competitive despite being simpler than multiscale variants; a good stability reference

architecture_summary: Separate before and after encoders with concat plus absolute-difference fusion at both bottleneck and skip scales
result_summary: Best separate-encoder result so far; rich explicit change representation helped, but it still trails the best shared bottleneck models
comparison_note: The 100-epoch follow-up underperformed this run, so this branch likely needs recipe changes rather than more training time

architecture_summary: Shared Siamese encoder with concat plus absolute-difference fusion at the bottleneck and decoder skip scales
result_summary: Highest Dice so far among all runs, but classification lag kept the joint score just outside the top tier
comparison_note: Promising segmentation-heavy direction; likely worth another pass only if classification can be lifted

architecture_summary: Shared Siamese encoder with concatenation at the bottleneck and skip scales; explicit multiscale change fusion
result_summary: Longer budget plus early stopping improved the multiscale shared model over its 20-epoch version
comparison_note: Still trails the best bottleneck models, so multiscale fusion is not yet paying off enough

architecture_summary: Shared Siamese encoder with simple bottleneck concatenation and increased classification loss weight
result_summary: Close to the leaders but worse than the same architecture with cls_weight=2.0
comparison_note: The heavier classification weighting hurt overall balance instead of improving it

architecture_summary: Post-disaster image only with a single encoder and no explicit before/after fusion; joint segmentation plus tile-level classification head
result_summary: A weaker rerun that slid back toward the middle of the pack despite using the same recipe
comparison_note: This variance makes after-only a useful baseline, but not the strongest main line for the next round

architecture_summary: Post-disaster image only with reduced classification weight and early stopping
result_summary: Improved segmentation relative to the original after-only winner but gave up too much classification strength
comparison_note: Lowering cls_weight moved the model away from the sweet spot

architecture_summary: Separate before and after encoders with concat plus absolute-difference fusion at the bottleneck only
result_summary: Reasonable bottleneck-only separate-encoder result, but it did not beat the better shared-encoder or separate-multiscale variants
comparison_note: Separate encoders still have upside, though multiscale fusion looks more promising than bottleneck-only here

architecture_summary: Shared Siamese encoder with concatenation at the bottleneck and decoder skip scales
result_summary: Original tuned multiscale winner before the latest batch of experiments
comparison_note: Useful historical checkpoint, but the 100-epoch early-stopped follow-up superseded it

architecture_summary: Post-disaster image only with increased classification weight and early stopping
result_summary: Another competitive after-only run, but still below the original cls_weight=2.0 setting
comparison_note: Increasing cls_weight above 2.0 did not improve the after-only recipe

architecture_summary: Original shared-encoder Siamese U-Net baseline with before and after features concatenated at the bottleneck and skip levels
result_summary: Best baseline-script result so far; longer training helped materially
comparison_note: Still behind the best change-family models, but it narrowed the gap

architecture_summary: Separate before and after encoders with concat plus absolute-difference fusion at both bottleneck and skip scales
result_summary: Longer training did not help the separate-encoder multiscale branch; both segmentation and classification slipped versus the earlier 50-epoch run
comparison_note: This branch looks inferior to the shared bottleneck recipe and should be deprioritized for now

architecture_summary: Original shared-encoder Siamese U-Net baseline with before and after features concatenated at the bottleneck and skip levels
result_summary: Strong baseline reference before the 50-epoch rerun
comparison_note: The 50-epoch rerun improved on this, confirming the baseline was undertrained at 20 epochs

architecture_summary: Shared Siamese encoder using only absolute-difference features at the bottleneck for change fusion
result_summary: Clear underperformer; absolute difference alone removed too much useful context
comparison_note: This result argues against pure absdiff fusion and in favor of keeping raw before/after features

architecture_summary: Early proof-of-concept version of the configurable change model with simple bottleneck concatenation
result_summary: Confirmed the configurable change-family pipeline worked end to end
comparison_note: Not directly comparable to later tuned runs because the budget and recipe were much weaker

architecture_summary: Initial shared-encoder Siamese U-Net baseline using Dice-based checkpoint selection
result_summary: Second short sanity-check baseline with slightly better classification than the first
comparison_note: Historical reference only; later tuned baselines and change-family runs overtook it

architecture_summary: Initial shared-encoder Siamese U-Net baseline using Dice-based checkpoint selection
result_summary: First successful end-to-end baseline sanity run
comparison_note: Important for project history, but it is no longer competitive
